skill.name: website-to-hyperframes
skill.description: Guides an AI agent through capturing a website and turning the captured brand, assets, script, voiceover, storyboard, and HyperFrames compositions into a previewable video project.
skill.summary: Create a HyperFrames video project from a captured website.
skill.author: heygen-com

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported a critical heuristic combination because the Markdown workflow includes external CLI commands, network capture, optional environment keys, and local file handling. Manual review found no prompt injection or confirmed malicious intent, and most Ruby backtick, weak cryptography, and path traversal hits are false positives from Markdown examples. The skill remains medium risk because its normal operation captures websites, downloads assets, runs HyperFrames commands, and can use optional cloud services.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Workflow Can Execute Local Tooling
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to run HyperFrames commands for capture, TTS, transcription, linting, validation, snapshots, preview, and rendering. This is legitimate for the video workflow, but it executes local tooling and should run only in a trusted project workspace.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit and central to the workflow. They appear purposeful rather than malicious, so the concern is execution risk, not confirmed compromise.

medium_findings.title: Website Capture Downloads Remote Content
medium_findings.description: The skill asks the agent to capture a user-provided URL and extract screenshots, fonts, text, and assets. This is expected behavior, but it can fetch untrusted pages and store third-party content locally.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network capture and local artifact creation are clearly documented. The user supplies the URL, which lowers maliciousness but keeps privacy and untrusted-content risk.

medium_findings.title: Optional Cloud Services May Process Captured Content
medium_findings.description: The workflow optionally uses Gemini vision for richer image descriptions and external TTS providers for narration. These services can receive captured site assets or script text when enabled by the user.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files explicitly mention optional API keys and external TTS tools. There is no evidence of secret exfiltration, but cloud processing is a real data-handling consideration.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Spans Flagged as Ruby Backticks
low_findings.description: The large external-command count comes from Markdown inline code and fenced examples. The files are documentation, not Ruby source executing backtick shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged files are Markdown reference documents and the surrounding context is instructional examples. No Ruby code execution construct was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Relative Asset Paths Flagged as Traversal
low_findings.description: Relative paths such as ../capture/assets are used to reference captured images, fonts, videos, and Lottie files from composition HTML. They do not instruct escaping to sensitive system paths.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The paths target the project capture directory and local generated assets. I found no evidence of reading arbitrary parent directories or sensitive files.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern Hits
low_findings.description: The weak-cryptography alerts do not correspond to security-sensitive cryptographic use. The reviewed references use deterministic animation helpers and Markdown examples, not password hashing or encryption.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence points to deterministic rendering patterns such as hash-like math and seeded randomness. These are not used for authentication, secrecy, or integrity protection.

dangerous_patterns.title: Runs HyperFrames Commands Through npx
dangerous_patterns.description: Multiple workflow steps ask the agent to run npx-based HyperFrames commands. This pattern executes installed or resolved Node tooling and should be reviewed in community skills.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The npx commands are directly present and intended to be run. They are relevant to the skill purpose but still constitute external command execution.

dangerous_patterns.title: Captures and Stores Website Screenshots and Assets
dangerous_patterns.description: The capture step downloads screenshots, fonts, text, and assets from a target website into the project. This can collect proprietary or sensitive content from pages the user captures.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The documentation explicitly names screenshots, downloaded assets, extracted text, fonts, and asset reuse. The risk is data handling rather than hidden behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: References Remote CDN Scripts in Composition Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The techniques reference includes example scripts loaded from jsDelivr for Lottie and GSAP MotionPath. These are normal frontend examples, but remote scripts add supply-chain and reproducibility risk.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The remote script URLs are explicit. They are examples for legitimate animation features, so the risk is moderate and contextual.

content.value_statement: Teams often need a quick video from an existing website, but manual capture, scripting, and motion design take time. This skill turns a URL into a structured HyperFrames video workflow with capture, storyboard, voiceover, compositions, validation, and preview.
actual_capabilities: Captures a website into screenshots, assets, fonts, extracted text, and design tokens.
Builds a concise design reference from captured brand colors, typography, components, and visual style.
Creates narration scripts and storyboard beats for social ads, product demos, teasers, and brand reels.
Guides voiceover generation, transcription, and beat timing from word-level timestamps.
Directs HyperFrames composition building with asset references, animation rules, captions, audio, snapshots, and preview validation.
limitations: Requires the HyperFrames CLI and compatible local project setup.
Captured website content may be subject to copyright, privacy, or access restrictions.
External TTS, Gemini vision, and CDN examples require user-approved network access and credentials.
Final MP4 rendering is intentionally on demand and can take several minutes.

use_cases.title: Launch a Product Promo
use_cases.description: Convert a product landing page into a short video ad with captured visuals, brand colors, narration, and a preview link.

use_cases.title: Create a Product Tour
use_cases.description: Turn a feature-heavy website into a structured demo video with storyboard beats, screenshots, captions, and motion treatments.

use_cases.title: Prototype Motion Concepts
use_cases.description: Use captured assets and HyperFrames techniques to test animated scenes, transitions, and visual systems before final production.

prompt_templates.title: Beginner Website Promo
prompt_templates.scenario: A user has one public website and wants a short social video.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 15-second social ad from this website: [URL]. Use the site visuals, write a concise script, and stop at the HyperFrames preview.

prompt_templates.title: Product Demo With Voiceover
prompt_templates.scenario: A user wants a narrated product walkthrough.
prompt_templates.prompt: Turn [URL] into a 30-second narrated product demo. Capture the site, write DESIGN.md, SCRIPT.md, and STORYBOARD.md, then build and validate the HyperFrames preview.

prompt_templates.title: Vertical Launch Teaser
prompt_templates.scenario: A user needs mobile-first launch content.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 10-second vertical launch teaser from [URL]. Use the strongest captured assets, a punchy hook, and motion that fits Instagram Stories or TikTok.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Brand Reel
prompt_templates.scenario: A user wants a detailed creative build with transitions and audio.
prompt_templates.prompt: Produce a 45-second brand reel from [URL]. Include a full asset audit, voice direction, beat timings, advanced animation techniques, snapshots, validation results, and a Studio preview URL.

output_examples.input: A startup landing page URL and a request for a 15-second launch video.
output_examples.output: A captured-site summary, brand reference, short narration script, beat-by-beat storyboard, generated narration timing, validated scenes, snapshots, and a HyperFrames Studio preview URL.

output_examples.input: A product documentation site and a request for a 30-second feature tour.
output_examples.output: A concise product story, selected screenshots and icons, scene directions for each feature, voiceover timing, animated composition guidance, and preview-ready project handoff.

output_examples.input: An ecommerce homepage and a request for a square social ad.
output_examples.output: A square-format video plan using product imagery, brand typography, animated offer text, music and SFX notes, validation checks, and optional render instructions.

best_practices: Use only URLs and captured content that the user is authorized to process.
Review the captured screenshots and asset descriptions before writing the script or storyboard.
Run lint, validation, and snapshot checks before sharing the Studio preview.
anti_patterns: Do not paste captured SVG or image file contents into prompts when a file path is enough.
Do not use live iframes of the captured website inside final compositions.
Do not render the MP4 until the user explicitly asks for export.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates a HyperFrames video project based on a captured website, including planning documents, compositions, validation steps, and a preview URL.

faq.question: Does it need API keys?
faq.answer: Basic capture does not require API keys. Optional Gemini vision, ElevenLabs, or HeyGen TTS features may require user-provided credentials.

faq.question: Can it work with private websites?
faq.answer: It can only capture content that the local browser or capture tool can access. Users must confirm they have permission to process that content.

faq.question: Does it automatically render an MP4?
faq.answer: No. The workflow delivers a preview first and renders an MP4 only when the user explicitly asks for export.

faq.question: Which formats are supported?
faq.answer: The workflow describes landscape, portrait, and square formats for demos, ads, teasers, product tours, and brand reels.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill is purpose-built and not malicious, but it runs local commands, captures remote pages, writes project files, and can call external services.
